task:
READ-ONLY DIAGNOSIS atom — DO NOT change any product code, configs, or governance files. The founder explicitly requires a diagnosis + proposed fix presented BEFORE any fix is made (ticket 0072). Your deliverable is a written diagnosis, not a code change.

BUG: Launching the priority 'local-cache-retention' (Machine-local cache retention — bound local/ growth with per-workspace run retention) fails with:
  'Launch needs attention. local-cache-retention — A run is already in flight for this workspace.'
This is a false positive: there is no actual run in flight. Prior fix attempts have failed, so diagnose to root cause before proposing anything.

DO THIS:
1. Find the exact source of the user-facing string 'A run is already in flight for this workspace' (grep the whole tree). Name the file:line that emits it.
2. Trace the full guard logic that decides 'a run is already in flight for this workspace': what state is read (lock file, in-memory map, daemon registry, run record on disk, counters.json, runs/ dir scan, status projection), where that state is written and cleared, and the exact predicate that trips. Name every file:line in the chain.
3. Identify WHY it false-positives specifically for local-cache-retention: is there a stale run record / lock that is never cleared after a prior run ended or faulted? A workspace-id vs priority-id mismatch? A crashed run leaving in-flight state? Reconcile against the actual on-disk state under local/runs/** and cocoder/runs/** and counters.json. Show the concrete stale artifact or the concrete logic flaw with file paths and contents.
4. Review the PRIOR fix attempts: run `git log --oneline -- <the guard files>` and read the diffs of commits that touched this guard; summarize what was tried and why each did not hold. This tells us which surface areas are involved.
5. Enumerate ALL surface areas the real fix would touch (the ticket warns it may touch many): guard logic, state lifecycle/cleanup, daemon, status projection, launch path, tests pinning the behavior.

RETURN a structured diagnosis as your final message (no file writes):
  - ROOT CAUSE: one paragraph, with the decisive file:line evidence.
  - WHY PRIOR FIXES FAILED: bullets tied to commits.
  - PROPOSED FIX: the specific change(s), each owner file, and what must not break.
  - SURFACE AREAS / RISK: list, with the tests that pin each.
  - VERIFICATION PLAN: the exact command(s) that would prove the fix (ideally a runnable proof harness).

Write-scope: NONE (read-only). Exit criterion (judgment-based): a complete, evidence-backed diagnosis with concrete file:line citations and a reproducible explanation of the stale/false in-flight state. Do not edit, do not commit.

FIX ticket 0072's defect class: the daemon's 'a run is already in flight' 409 carries no structured code, the UI bridge drops the code, and the UI classifies the error with a fragile /in flight/i regex. Replace the regex classification with a structured daemon code, and pin the independent-of-runner launch routing with a regression test so a stale/regressed renderer can't silently route a runnerless priority back through /runs. DO NOT weaken or remove the daemon single-writer in-flight guard (ctx.inFlight) — it is the correct launch lock; keep it.

WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/**, packages/ui/**, and their tests only. No governance files, no priorities, no docs beyond code comments.

MAKE THESE CHANGES (all owners — re-grep to confirm each before editing):

1. DAEMON — add a structured code + the blocking runId to BOTH in-flight 409 bodies:
   - packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:764 (launchRun guard) and packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:1171 (resumeRun guard).
   - New body shape, matching the existing coded 409s at lines 826/840: { error: <keep the existing human string>, code: 'workspace-in-flight', runId: <the active blocking run id> }. The active id is ctx.inFlight.get(workspaceId) (at 764) / ctx.inFlight.get(run.workspaceId) (at 1171); when it is the literal 'pending' reservation, set runId: null (do not leak the sentinel). Keep the error string so any old consumer still works.

2. UI BRIDGE — stop dropping the structured code: packages/ui/src/main/daemon-client.ts. Add an optional `code?: string` (and pass through `runId` if cheaply available) to the failure branch at line ~92 ({ ok: false, status, error, code: r.payload?.code }). Add `code?: string` to the MutationResult/failure type (around line 20-26) so it type-checks end to end.

3. UI CLASSIFIERS — branch on the structured code, not the regex: packages/ui/src/renderer/App.tsx:604 (doLaunch) and App.tsx:635 (doLaunchTicket). Change `res.status === 409 && /in flight/i.test(res.error)` to `res.status === 409 && res.code === 'workspace-in-flight'`. Keep the founder-facing message; if a blocking runId is present, you may include it (e.g. 'A run is already in flight for this workspace (run <id>).'), otherwise keep the existing wording. Do NOT change the independent-of-runner handoff routing at App.tsx:586 — it is correct.

4. REGRESSION TEST (the key durability guard) — add a UI launch-path test (packages/ui/tests/, alongside live-app.test.tsx) asserting that launching a priority with independentOfRunner === true (a local-cache-retention-shaped fixture) calls launchIndependentHandoff / POSTs /runs/independent-handoff and NEVER calls launchRun / POSTs /runs. This pins commit 39fa6df's routing so a regression can't reintroduce the original bug.

5. UPDATE EXISTING TESTS to the new contract: packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts (the 'POST /runs → 409 when a run is already in flight' test, ~line 1891) must assert the new body carries code: 'workspace-in-flight'. Check packages/cli/tests/client.test.ts and any UI test asserting the old regex/message and update them. Do NOT weaken an assertion into a tautology — assert the real code and the real routing.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST NOT BREAK:
- The daemon in-flight guard still blocks a real concurrent launch (single-writer preserved); only the 409 BODY gains a code + runId.
- UI no longer uses /in flight/i to classify; it uses res.code === 'workspace-in-flight'.
- independentOfRunner priorities still route to the handoff, never /runs (pinned by the new test).
- Run and report exit codes for: `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test`, `pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui test`, and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon --filter @cocoder/ui typecheck`. All must be green, with the NEW tests present and passing. Report the actual pass counts and exit codes.

EXIT CRITERION (judgment + scripted): all four owner areas changed, the new independent-of-runner routing regression test added and passing, updated daemon/cli tests asserting the new code, and the three commands above green by real exit code.
